foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe All Round Gothic as a geometric sans-serif originally designed in 2012 by Ryoichi Tsunekawa, inspired by Avant Garde Gothic and Futura. They highlight its imperfectly circular bowls and removal of unnecessary stems as the source of its soft, friendly character, and note its use in the Minions movie as evidence of its broad appeal across text, headlines, logos, posters and animation.

name: Great Victorian
classification_notes: A decorative revival based on Euclid and Euclid Initials from the Cleveland Type Foundry, with the ornate, heavily embellished character typical of Victorian-era display faces.
summary: A decorative revival of Euclid and Euclid Initials, two ornate display faces originally issued by the Cleveland Type Foundry in the late 19th century.
description: Based on Euclid and Euclid Initials from the Cleveland Type Foundry, this revival brings the elaborate embellishment of Victorian-era display typography into digital form. The ornate detailing reflects the craft traditions of 19th-century type design, where letterforms were treated as objects of decorative art. Best suited to vintage-inspired branding, packaging and editorial work where period atmosphere is the priority.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Great Victorian as a decorative typeface based on Euclid and Euclid Initials, originally issued by the Cleveland Type Foundry in the late 19th century, capturing the ornate elegance and craftsmanship of Victorian-era typography and making it ideal for vintage-inspired designs.

name: Antoinette Monogrammes
classification_notes: A decorative script font designed specifically for monogram creation, featuring upright and slanted script capitals alongside ornate frames, placing it firmly in the display script category.
summary: A monogram font drawn from early 20th-century embroidery designs, offering upright and slanted script capitals alongside 24 ornate frames.
description: Inspired by Janon Co. embroidery designs from the early 1900s, the font maps upright script capitals to uppercase keys, slanted variants to lowercase, and ornate frames to numbers and symbols. This split-key approach lets users layer characters in professional vector software to build finished monograms. Well suited to wedding stationery, custom stamps and logo work where a warm, period-craft aesthetic is wanted.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Antoinette Monogrammes as a monogram font inspired by early 20th-century embroidery designs by Janon Co., featuring upright script capitals, slanted script capitals and 24 ornate frames for creating personalised monograms with a handcrafted feel. They recommend use in wedding invitations, scrapbooks, custom stamps and logos, and note that professional design software such as Adobe Illustrator is required for assembly.

name: FontAid V
classification_notes: A charity collaborative font project featuring an antiqued Kanji character contribution; best classified as display given the decorative, illustrative nature of the contribution.
summary: A charity typeface collaboration produced in aid of Japanese relief organisations, featuring a contribution from Dharma Type of an antiqued Kanji character for 'Samurai'.
description: FontAid V is a collaborative font project organised by SOTA (Society of Typographic Aficionados), with Dharma Type contributing an antiqued Kanji character representing 'Samurai'. All proceeds from sales go directly to Japanese relief organisations including AMDA International. The font is available through selected vendors listed on the SOTA website.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type contributed an antiqued Kanji letter representing 'Samurai' to FontAid V, a collaborative charity font project. The font is sold through selected vendors on the SOTA website, with all proceeds going to support organisations in Japan such as AMDA International.

name: Dharma Gothic
classification_notes: An antiqued condensed sans-serif inspired by 19th-century wood type, placing it firmly in the display category with a historical vernacular subcategory rather than a functional grotesque or humanist classification.
summary: A condensed antiqued sans-serif inspired by 19th-century wood type, offered in 42 styles for poster and headline work.
description: Drawn from the vernacular of 19th-century wood type, this condensed family leans into retro texture and period character across 42 styles. Alternate glyphs for g, r and y are available via OpenType salt, and tabular figures can be switched on for numeric setting. A companion set called Gothic Extras provides borders, ornaments and frames drawn from vintage Hamilton wood-type catalogues of the 1800s, offered free alongside the main family.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Dharma Gothic as an antiqued sans serif inspired by 19th-century wood type, with each glyph crafted to evoke a retro, nostalgic aesthetic. The condensed family spans 42 styles suited to posters and titles, and is accompanied by the free Gothic Extras set of borders, ornaments and frames inspired by vintage Hamilton catalogs. OpenType features include alternate glyphs via salt and tabular figures via tnum.

name: Dharma Slab
classification_notes: A condensed slab serif explicitly inspired by 1800s wood type, placing it firmly in the display slab category with a wood type revival subcategory.
summary: A condensed display slab serif drawing on 1800s wood type traditions, offered in 42 styles for poster and titling work.
description: Built around the visual language of nineteenth-century wood type, this family delivers the weathered, high-impact quality of antique letterpress printing in a contemporary digital package. Forty-two styles give considerable range across weights and italics, with OpenType alternates for g, r and y adding typographic flexibility. A companion set of borders, ornaments and frames based on vintage Hamilton catalogues is available free to round out period-style layouts.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Dharma Slab as an antiqued slab serif inspired by 1800s wood type, crafted to evoke nostalgia through retro letterforms. The condensed family spans 42 styles suited to posters and titles, with OpenType features including stylistic alternates and tabular figures. A free companion family, Gothic Extras, provides borders, ornaments and frames drawn from vintage Hamilton catalogues.

name: Rama Gothic
classification_notes: A condensed antiqued sans serif explicitly inspired by 19th-century wood type, placing it firmly in the display category as a revival of wood type vernacular rather than a general-use grotesque.
summary: A condensed antiqued display sans built on 19th-century wood type references, spanning 18 styles for poster and headline work.
description: Drawn from the visual grammar of 19th-century wood type, each glyph is treated with an antiqued quality that calls back to the era of letterpress printing and Hamilton Manufacturing catalogues. The family runs to 18 styles with a consistently tight, condensed proportion suited to posters, titles and packaging where strong vertical rhythm is needed. A companion set, Gothic Extras, bundles period-appropriate borders, ornaments and frames at no cost.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Rama Gothic as an antiqued sans serif typeface inspired by 19th-century wood type, with each glyph carefully crafted to evoke the retro aesthetic and vintage charm of that era. The condensed family comprises 18 styles suited to posters and titles, and is accompanied by the free Gothic Extras family of borders, ornaments and frames drawn from Hamilton's vintage 1800s catalogues. An alternate glyph for the letter 'r' is accessible via OpenType stylistic alternates.

name: Rama Slab
classification_notes: A condensed slab serif explicitly inspired by 19th-century wood type, placing it firmly in the display slab category with strong historical vernacular roots.
summary: A condensed, antiqued slab serif drawn from 19th-century wood type, offered in 18 styles for poster and headline use.
description: Each glyph carries deliberate period texture to evoke the visual weight and worn charm of letterpress wood type. The family runs 18 condensed styles, suited to posters, titles and display work where bold impact is the priority. An OpenType stylistic alternate for the letter 'r' adds a small layer of typographic flexibility.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Rama Slab as an antiqued slab serif inspired by 19th-century wood type, with each glyph crafted to evoke the retro charm and nostalgia of that era. The condensed family spans 18 styles and includes an alternate 'r' glyph via the OpenType stylistic alternates feature, making it suited to posters, titles and bold display applications.

name: Gothic Extras
classification_notes: A decorative ornament and border collection rather than a text typeface; classified as display due to its purely supplementary and decorative function in typographic composition.
summary: A companion ornament set for Dharma Type's Gothic and Slab families, drawing on Hamilton wood type catalogue borders and frames from the 1800s.
description: Designed to work alongside Dharma Gothic, Dharma Slab, Rama Gothic and Rama Slab, this collection provides borders, ornaments and frames for period-styled typographic layouts. The source material is Hamilton's vintage wood type catalogues from the nineteenth century, lending the set a distinctly Victorian flavour. Useful for posters, packaging and editorial work where decorative framing is needed.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Gothic Extras as a complement to their Gothic and Slab typefaces, comprising borders, ornaments and frames inspired by vintage Hamilton catalogues from the 1800s, intended to add a classic and decorative touch to typographic compositions.

name: Dharma Gothic P
classification_notes: Based on Dharma Gothic, a condensed grotesque, but treated with deliberate distressing to simulate aged, ink-worn letterpress print. The worn texture and vintage character push it firmly into display territory.
summary: A distressed version of Dharma Gothic that simulates the ink-worn texture of aged letterpress printing across four styles.
description: Built on the skeleton of Dharma Gothic, this variant applies deliberate hand-tweaked distressing to each glyph, replicating the ink-splotched irregularities of worn vintage print. The family covers Roman, Italic, Bold and Bold Italic, giving workable range for headline and display use. Stylistic alternates via OpenType salt feature offer variant forms for characters such as g, r and y.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Dharma Gothic P as a distressed variation of the original Dharma Gothic, with each glyph hand-tweaked to capture the texture and imperfections of aged print, inspired by worn, ink-splotched vintage printing. The family includes Roman, Italic, Bold and Bold Italic styles, with OpenType stylistic alternates available for select characters.

name: Mocha Mattari
classification_notes: An all-caps distressed typeface derived from Bebas Neue, with intentional damage and manual alterations giving it a worn, tactile character suited to bold display use.
summary: A deliberately worn all-caps display font derived from Bebas Neue, with hand-damaged letterforms and OpenType ligatures for common unit abbreviations.
description: Built on the skeleton of Bebas Neue, the letterforms have been physically damaged and manually reworked to produce a gritty, tactile surface. The family is all-caps but includes a set of alternate uppercase glyphs, with discretionary ligatures covering shorthand unit combinations such as g, oz and lb. Suited to rough-edged poster work, packaging and any setting where clean geometric caps would feel too polished.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Mocha Mattari as a distressed display font based on the widely popular Bebas Neue, originally released as a free font in 2010. The worn aesthetic was created by intentionally damaging and manually tweaking the original design, resulting in a gritty, tactile feel. While it omits lowercase letters, it includes alternative uppercase glyphs and OpenType support for unit-abbreviation ligatures via dlig and salt features.

name: Shiva
classification_notes: Shiva is a highly condensed sans-serif with very thin strokes, designed for both text and titling use. Its extreme condensation and thin construction place it firmly in the condensed display sans category.
summary: A highly condensed, thin-stroked sans-serif designed for both text setting and titling, with stylistic alternates for key characters.
description: Despite extremely thin strokes, the design maintains a strong and dignified presence, making it suited to both body text and headline work at narrow column widths. Key characters including a, e, f, g, and y carry alternate glyphs accessible via the OpenType stylistic alternates feature, giving designers additional typographic options. The italic also includes alternates for g and y, extending the customisation further.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Shiva as a highly condensed typeface designed for both text and titling. They note that despite its very thin strokes it maintains a strong, impactful and dignified presence on the page, and highlight OpenType stylistic alternates for key characters in both Roman and Italic.

name: Speedometer
classification_notes: A narrow, high-contrast titling face with extreme width compression and strong stroke contrast, placing it firmly in the condensed display category suited to logos and headlines.
summary: A narrow, high-contrast display face derived from Dharma Type's Dimensions family, built for titling and logo work.
description: Derived from Dimensions, one of Dharma Type's narrowest and boldest families, Speedometer introduces pronounced contrast between thick stems and thin arms to sharpen its sense of energy. Six weights run from Thin to Black with matching italics, and small capitals are accessible via the OpenType smcp feature. Best suited to posters, logotypes and headline settings where a condensed, dynamic silhouette is required.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Speedometer as derived from the Dimensions font family, noting it as one of the narrowest and boldest fonts designed for titling and logos. They highlight its striking contrast between thick stems and thin arms as delivering a more elegant appearance than Dimensions while enhancing a sense of energy and speed.

name: Dimensions
classification_notes: An extremely condensed, multi-weight display family rooted in a bold blackout aesthetic, designed for logos and identity work rather than extended text setting.
summary: An extremely condensed display family evolved from the free Blackout font, offering seven weights with italics and small capitals for identity and logo work.
description: Originally released as the free Blackout font in 2005, the family was reworked and expanded into Dimensions in 2011, gaining a full range of seven weights each with roman and italic styles. It sits at the extreme end of condensed type, making it well suited to logotypes, fashion branding and music industry identities where vertical emphasis is wanted. OpenType small capitals are included across all styles, extending its typographic range beyond headline use.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Dimensions as a redesigned family based on the original Blackout font, which first appeared as a free release in 2005 and found particular traction with fashion and music brands. The expanded 2011 family spans seven weights in both roman and italic, and is positioned as one of the most condensed, bold and skinny font families available. All styles include uppercase, lowercase, accented characters and small capitals via the OpenType smcp feature.

name: Onick
classification_notes: Described as a free font designed specifically for Onitsuka Tiger, suggesting a custom grotesque or geometric sans created for brand use; insufficient detail to determine precise subcategory.
summary: A custom typeface designed by Ian Lynam specifically for Onitsuka Tiger, made available as a free download via Dharma Type.
description: Commissioned for the Japanese sportswear brand Onitsuka Tiger, Onick is a purpose-built typeface by Ian Lynam released as a free font. The design is detailed further in an article by Lynam, suggesting considered rationale behind its creation. Available as a single style download from Dharma Type.
foundry_blurb: Onick is a free font designed specifically for Onitsuka Tiger. Dharma Type directs visitors to Ian Lynam's article for further details on the typeface.

name: Design System
classification_notes: A square, geometric sans-serif with a 1970s influence, spanning five weights and seven widths from condensed to ultra-wide; clean minimal forms place it firmly in the geometric sans tradition.
summary: A 70-style square geometric sans spanning five weights and seven widths, built for mixing across titles, logos and text.
description: Covering five weights and seven widths from ultra condensed to ultra wide, the family gives designers 70 harmoniously drawn styles to combine freely. The square, minimal forms carry a faint 1970s flavour without leaning heavily on retro styling. All widths and weights are intended to work together, making it practical for variable-width layouts and multi-weight typographic systems.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Design System as a versatile type system of 70 font styles built from five weights, seven widths and two slopes. They note it spans 70s-style simple square sans forms through to ultra-wide designs, suited to titles, logos and text, with all styles crafted to mix and match without visual dissonance.

name: Bebas Neue
classification_notes: A tall, condensed all-caps sans-serif display face built for large-scale titling and headline use, with no lowercase glyphs.
summary: A free all-caps condensed display typeface by Ryoichi Tsunekawa, designed for headlines and large-scale titling work.
description: Released under the SIL Open Font License, this all-caps sans has become one of the most widely used free display faces on the web. Its tall, tight letterforms suit posters, titles and banner-scale applications where a strong vertical rhythm is needed. The single weight limits flexibility but the face excels within its narrow brief.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Bebas Neue as a free display font designed by Ryoichi Tsunekawa, suited to headlines, captions and titling, and released under the SIL Open Font License v1.1.

name: Stereo Gothic
classification_notes: A wide, undecorated sans-serif with a range of weights, fitting squarely into the Gothic sans tradition with its emphasis on legibility and clean functional form.
summary: A wide, undecorated Gothic sans focused on legibility across a range of weights.
description: Built around a wide proportion with no ornamental detail, Stereo Gothic prioritises clean, functional letterforms. The range of weights gives it practical flexibility for display and identity work. Its straightforward construction makes it an easy choice where clarity and breadth are the primary requirements.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Stereo Gothic as a simple, straightforward design: wide, highly legible, and free of decoration. Its weight range adds flexibility for a variety of design needs.

name: Sneaker Script
classification_notes: A flowing connected script with looping letterforms and retro stylistic alternates via OpenType, evoking informal hand-lettered styles of the 1960s and 70s.
summary: A looping connected script with retro character, drawing on the flowing hand-lettered aesthetic of the 1960s and 70s.
description: The letterforms curl and swirl with exuberant energy, supported by OpenType alternates that extend the range of stylistic variation. Its informal, flowing rhythm suits display and packaging work where a vintage, hand-crafted quality is wanted. The retro sensibility sits comfortably in branding, posters and apparel graphics.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Sneaker Script as looping and swirling like spaghetti, powered by OpenType alternates. They note its flowing, retro charm evokes the spirit of the 1960s and 70s, bringing back the energy and style of that era.

name: Concrete Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil-style script typeface offered in clean and distressed variants; the stencil construction and script letterforms place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A stencil script offered in two cuts: a clean precision version and a distressed, effected variant.
description: Two styles cover the range from sharp, precise stencil letterforms to a rougher, textured cut with deliberate edge. OpenType titling alternates extend typographic options beyond the default character set. Suited to packaging, signage and campaign work where a hand-stencilled aesthetic is the intent.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type position Concrete Stencil as the definitive stencil script, available in Clean and Stencil-Effected styles for precision or grit, with OpenType titling alternates for added typographic flexibility.

name: African Elephant Trunk
classification_notes: A decorative display face drawn from the aesthetic of mid-20th century vinyl record lettering, placing it firmly in the display category with a retro vernacular subcategory.
summary: A retro display face drawn from early to mid-20th century vinyl record lettering, with small caps included for typographic flexibility.
description: Modelled on the hand-lettered and typeset styles found on vinyl records from the 1920s through to the 1950s, the face carries a warmth associated with that era of print and packaging. Small caps are included, giving it a degree of typographic versatility beyond simple headline use. Best suited to music-adjacent branding, event posters and editorial contexts where a period aesthetic is called for.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe African Elephant Trunk as inspired by retro vinyl records from the early to mid-20th century, capturing a nostalgic yet timeless aesthetic. The font includes small caps for enhanced flexibility in advanced typography.

name: Balaghat
classification_notes: A hand-lettered flowing cursive that mimics smooth brush strokes, placing it firmly in the brush script subcategory of script typefaces.
summary: A flowing brush-script with smooth cursive strokes, aimed at logotypes, editorial headlines and print ephemera.
description: Designed to evoke the movement of hand-lettering with a natural, ink-on-paper quality, Balaghat suits high-end logotypes and magazine headlines as much as greeting cards and book covers. Its cursive rhythm keeps it versatile across print and screen contexts. The single weight keeps the offering focused, relying entirely on the character of the letterforms.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Balaghat as a decorative yet natural hand-lettered script whose most distinctive feature is its flowing cursive form, reminiscent of smooth brush strokes. They recommend it for high-end logotypes, magazine headlines, greeting cards, invitations, posters, book covers, advertisements and web applications.

name: Holiday Type Project
classification_notes: The collection consists of script typefaces modelled on hand-painted shopfront lettering and wall advertisements, placing it firmly in the script category with a retro vernacular subcategory.
summary: A collection of seventeen retro script faces drawn from 1950s Italian shopfront lettering and hand-painted wall advertisements.
description: Each face in the collection takes its name from a type of Italian shop or establishment, evoking the hand-lettered signage of mid-century streets. The designs blend a handcrafted looseness with the elegant curves typical of Italian commercial lettering from the period. Best suited to packaging, branding and display work that calls for a nostalgic Mediterranean flavour.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe the Holiday Type Project as retro script fonts inspired by shopfront lettering and hand-painted wall advertisements from 1950s Italy, capturing the nostalgic charm of mid-century Italian streets and blending elegance with a handcrafted feel.

name: Pixel Font Project
classification_notes: These are deliberately constructed 8-bit pixel fonts built on a fixed grid, placing them firmly in the display/bitmap category with no continuous stroke forms.
summary: A collection of 8-bit pixel fonts from Dharma Type, drawing on 1980s game design and mid-1990s pixel font conventions across fourteen stylistically varied subfamilies.
description: Each subfamily adapts a distinct historical reference point, from Art Deco and Fraktur to Gothic and stencil, rendered on a strict pixel grid at 10 or 20 units. The fonts are intended to be set at exact multiples of those sizes with anti-aliasing disabled to preserve the hard-edged, retro character. The range spans the Flat10 and Flat20 series alongside two Vigesimals entries, giving designers a broad palette within a consistent pixel-grid system.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe the Pixel Font Project as 8-bit pixel fonts crafted in homage to 1980s game designers and the pixel font pioneers of the mid-1990s, recommending use at 10 or 20 pixels, or their multiples, with anti-aliasing switched off for a sharp retro feel.

name: Prop-a-ganda project
classification_notes: Bold, decorative letterforms drawn from mid-century propaganda posters, vintage advertisements and retro packaging place this firmly in the display category with a retro vernacular subcategory.
summary: A collection of display typefaces drawn from mid-century propaganda posters, vintage advertisements and retro packaging from around the world.
description: Prop-a-ganda is a series of display fonts by Dharma Type that channel the bold lettering traditions of global mid-century graphic design. The collection draws on sources including vintage propaganda posters, classic advertisements and retro packaging, aiming to capture the graphic directness of that era. Best suited to editorial work, packaging or branding projects that call for a period-specific flavour.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Prop-a-ganda as retro-flavoured fonts inspired by lettering found on vintage propaganda posters, classic advertisements and retro packaging from around the world, capturing the bold spirit and nostalgic charm of global mid-century design.

name: Yasashii
classification_notes: An Art Deco display typeface drawing on Japanese cosmetic packaging and poster lettering from the late 19th to early 20th century; the decorative, structured character places it firmly in the display category with an art deco subcategory.
summary: An Art Deco display face drawing on Japanese cosmetic packaging and poster lettering from the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
description: Rooted in the decorative lettering found on Japanese cosmetics and posters of the Meiji and Taisho eras, Yasashii pairs geometric structure with delicate detailing characteristic of Art Deco design. The result sits somewhere between the formal rigidity of Western Art Deco and the refined ornamental sensibility of Japanese commercial art. Well suited to branding, packaging and editorial work where a sense of period elegance is required.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Yasashii as an Art Deco-inspired font rooted in Japanese design aesthetics, drawing on cosmetic packaging and posters from the late 19th to early 20th century. They characterise it as delicate yet structured, with a nostalgic charm suited to modern design.

name: Rouge
classification_notes: Highly abstracted letterforms inspired by smeared lip rouge make this a display typeface with no clear structural lineage; organic yet geometric qualities place it firmly in expressive or experimental display territory.
summary: An abstract display face with letterforms derived from the smeared trace of lip rouge on glass.
description: The design takes its starting point from a literal image: lip rouge smeared across a windscreen, then abstracted into letterforms. The resulting shapes sit somewhere between organic and geometric, and the foundry notes that different viewers read them differently, some finding them natural, others mathematical or archaic. Best suited to editorial headlines, packaging or identity work where ambiguity and emotional charge are assets.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Rouge as a unique typeface originally inspired by the image of lip rouge smeared on a windscreen. They note that its abstract forms evoke different impressions depending on the viewer, with some finding them organic, others mathematical or even ancient, framing it as a font that invites interpretation and emotional response.

name: Perfect Magic
classification_notes: A display serif with decorative, period-flavoured letterforms drawn from swing-era jazz album cover typography, placing it firmly in the display/decorative serif category.
summary: A display serif drawn from swing-era jazz album cover lettering, carrying a playful vintage rhythm suited to packaging and children's publishing.
description: Inspired by early 20th-century jazz album cover typography, Perfect Magic brings a loose, decorative quality to display work. The period-inflected letterforms sit comfortably in children's picture books, product packaging and any project where nostalgic warmth is the aim. A single weight limits its range, but within decorative display use the character is consistent and well-defined.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Perfect Magic as a jazzy serif typeface inspired by swing-era jazz album covers from the early 20th century, with a playful rhythm and vintage charm well suited to children's picture books, packaging and designs calling for nostalgic flair.

name: Banana
classification_notes: Heavy, rounded letterforms with wide proportions and upright stems place this firmly in the display category, specifically a rounded display style suited to bold, friendly headlines.
summary: A heavy rounded display face with wide letterforms and upright stems that projects a calm, approachable character.
description: Wide proportions and thick, rounded shapes give this face a grounded, unhurried quality suited to friendly branding and bold display settings. The upright stems avoid the aggression common to heavy display types, keeping the tone approachable rather than confrontational. Works well where weight is needed without severity.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Banana as a typeface that leaves a strong impression through heavy, rounded shapes. Wide letterforms and upright stems create a sense of ease and calm, lending the face a steady, approachable, and unhurried character.

name: Nothing
classification_notes: A heavy, fast-flowing handwriting script with brush-like strokes, categorised as a brush script due to its spontaneous, connected letterforms evocative of mid-century American lettering traditions.
summary: A heavy brush script by Ryoichi Tsunekawa that won the MyFonts No.1 Award-Winning Font of 2007, drawing on American lettering traditions from a Nagoya studio.
description: Designed by Ryoichi Tsunekawa and released in August 2006, Nothing combines wide, fast-flowing strokes with a confident, connected rhythm that evokes mid-century Hollywood and Madison Avenue hand-lettering. Despite its spontaneous appearance, the letterforms maintain a technical simplicity and balance that makes it accessible for expressive display work. Its commercial success and award recognition reflect its broad appeal across branding, poster and title applications.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Nothing as a true handwriting script making a powerful visual impact through its heavy, wide and fast-flowing strokes. MyFonts honoured it as their No.1 Award-Winning Font of 2007, and their editorial copy notes it feels spontaneous yet balanced, confidently connected yet technically simple, evoking the work of great American lettering artists despite originating from Flat-It's studio in Nagoya, Japan.

name: Word From Radio
classification_notes: A decorative display face drawing on mid-century vinyl record lettering, combining funky, psychedelic and space-age influences typical of 1960s–70s graphic design.
summary: A display face modelled on mid-20th century vinyl record lettering, mixing psychedelic energy with space-age graphic style.
description: Inspired by the hand-lettering found on retro vinyl record sleeves, the design channels the funky, forward-looking spirit of 1960s and 70s album art. The result sits somewhere between hippie psychedelia and mid-century futurism, making it suited to posters, record-related branding and retro-themed campaigns. Best used at large sizes where its decorative character can be read clearly.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Word From Radio as based on retro vinyl records from the mid-20th century, blending funky energy, hippie spirit and mid-century futuristic style into a lively mix of nostalgia and forward-thinking design, suited to bold, retro-inspired creations.

name: Rebel Train Goes
classification_notes: A decorative display face drawing on mid-20th century psychedelic and vinyl record aesthetics, placing it firmly in the display category with a retro subcategory.
summary: A retro display face drawing on mid-20th century vinyl record culture, blending psychedelic lettering with mid-century futuristic styling.
description: Designed to evoke the visual language of 1960s and 70s vinyl sleeve graphics, the typeface channels funky, hippie-era letterforms with a touch of space-age optimism. It suits posters, album artwork, event branding and any project that leans into vintage counterculture without abandoning legibility. The single style keeps the offering focused on its core decorative purpose.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Rebel Train Goes as inspired by retro vinyl records from the mid-20th century, combining funky flair, hippie vibes and mid-century futuristic aesthetics into a bold, nostalgic face suited to designs that celebrate vintage cool with a modern twist.

name: Moon Star Soul
classification_notes: A decorative display face with strong period styling drawn from mid-century vinyl record aesthetics, funk and hippie culture, placing it firmly in the retro display category.
summary: A bold retro display face modelled on mid-20th century vinyl record lettering, with a flavour of funk and hippie-era graphic design.
description: Drawn from the visual language of mid-century vinyl records, the typeface mixes funk, counterculture and futurist styling into a single decorative face. It is aimed squarely at projects that want an authentic period flavour without reaching for stock pastiche. The bold weight and nostalgic character suit poster work, album artwork and branding with a retro brief.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Moon Star Soul as a bold and nostalgic typeface inspired by retro vinyl records from the mid-20th century, blending elements of funk, hippie culture and mid-century futurism to capture the spirit of a colourful era.

name: Homemade Text
classification_notes: A handwritten-style text font designed to evoke informal, pen-drawn lettering at readable text sizes, placing it firmly in the script/handwritten category.
summary: A handwritten text face designed as a companion to Dharma Type's Grandes Vacances and Mademoiselle, optimised for legibility at smaller sizes.
description: Pitched as a companion to Dharma Type's Grandes Vacances and Mademoiselle, this face brings an informal, pen-drawn quality to running text rather than display use. The emphasis on legibility sets it apart from more expressive script faces, making it suited to greeting cards, editorial accents and packaging where a warm, handwritten tone is needed. Little detail is available on the weight range or language support beyond the core Latin character set.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Homemade Text as a companion to their Grandes Vacances and Mademoiselle typefaces, calling it a delightfully sweet and highly legible text font ideal for bringing a warm, handwritten feel to designs.

name: Mademoiselle
classification_notes: A decorative Tuscan display typeface in the Wood Type / Wild West tradition, characterised by the bifurcated serifs and ornamental detail typical of 19th-century poster lettering.
summary: A Tuscan wood type revival with a distinctly playful character, drawing on Wild West poster lettering traditions.
description: Sitting within the Tuscan display tradition, this family takes its cues from 19th-century wood type and the ornamental lettering of American Wild West poster design. The bifurcated, cupped serifs typical of the style are rendered with a lightness that sets it apart from more rugged revivals. Suited to packaging, branding and editorial work where decorative warmth is the priority.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Mademoiselle as one of the most unique Wild West-inspired Tuscan fonts available, noting that no other decorative wood type or Tuscan-style font offers the same level of cuteness and charm as this font family.

name: Grandes Vacances
classification_notes: A decorative Tuscan display face in the Wild West wood type tradition, characterised by bifurcated serifs and ornamental letterforms typical of 19th-century showbill typography.
summary: A decorative Tuscan display face in the Wild West wood type tradition, described as charming and playful with bifurcated, ornamental serifs.
description: Grandes Vacances draws on the 19th-century Tuscan wood type tradition, with the bifurcated serifs and showbill character typical of Western-style display printing. Dharma Type position it as one of the more distinctive entries in the genre, emphasising a sweetness and charm that sets it apart from harder-edged western display faces. Suited to posters, packaging and branding that calls for a vintage, fairground or frontier flavour.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Grandes Vacances as one of the most distinctive Wild West-style Tuscan fonts available, a bold, playful and irresistibly cute wood type family with a charm and sweetness they say no other decorative Tuscan quite matches.

name: Dremie
classification_notes: Dremie is a display typeface in the Art Deco tradition, characterised by geometric construction, crisp edges and layering capability suited to titling rather than text.
summary: A geometric Art Deco display face with crisp edges and Open and Fill layering styles for colour work.
description: Built around simple geometric forms in the Art Deco tradition, Dremie is designed for titling and display use where clean, hard-edged letterforms are needed. The family includes Open and Fill variants that can be stacked in layers to produce colour and depth effects without additional drawing. A straightforward choice for poster, packaging or headline work that calls for period-adjacent geometry.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Dremie as a basic Art Deco-style font with simple, crisp edges, suited to titling and display design, with Open and Fill styles that can be combined in layers to create striking colour effects and visual depth.

name: Garash Script
classification_notes: Explicitly a script typeface with highly decorative, ornate letterforms inspired by vintage Eastern European lettering, placing it firmly in the decorative script subcategory.
summary: An ornate decorative script drawn from vintage Eastern European lettering traditions, aimed at playful and illustrative applications.
description: The letterforms take their cues from the ornate hand-lettering found in vintage Eastern European print and packaging. The style suits picture books, children's product design and any application where a warm, nostalgic hand-drawn quality is needed. Its decorative character makes it a display-first choice rather than a workhorse text face.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Garash Script as highly decorative, inspired by vintage lettering from Eastern Europe, and recommend it for picture books, children's toys and other playful, eye-catching designs.

name: Garash
classification_notes: Classified as script due to its hand-lettering origins; the decorative and ornate qualities inspired by vintage Eastern European lettering place it firmly in the decorative script subcategory.
summary: An ornate decorative script drawn from vintage Eastern European lettering traditions, suited to picture books and playful packaging.
description: Garash takes its cues from the ornamental hand-lettering styles found in Eastern European vintage design, translating that heritage into a display script with charm and visual weight. Its heavily decorated forms make it well-suited to children's publishing, toy branding and any application where an eye-catching, characterful headline is needed. The style leans nostalgic and fairytale-adjacent rather than formal or calligraphic.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Garash as a highly decorative script inspired by vintage lettering from Eastern Europe, noting its charming and ornate style as ideal for picture books, children's toys and other playful, eye-catching designs.

name: Xesy
classification_notes: Xesy is described as whimsical and quirky, placing it firmly in the casual script category with a playful, hand-drawn character rather than a formal or calligraphic tradition.
summary: A quirky casual script with a playful, hand-drawn quality suited to cheerful, lighthearted design work.
description: Xesy is a casual script with an intentionally odd, bouncy character designed for upbeat and festive contexts. Its irregular letterforms give it a hand-drawn feel that fits greeting cards, event graphics and informal branding. The family appears to offer a single style with no weight range or companion cuts.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Xesy as a whimsical and quirky script font made for happy, fun-filled moments, noting that its odd and playful style brings a lighthearted touch to any cheerful design.

name: Deluta Black
classification_notes: Explicitly a blackletter font; described as playful and quirky, suggesting a decorative or display-oriented take on traditional blackletter forms.
summary: A playful, decorative blackletter from Dharma Type that puts a light-hearted spin on traditional gothic lettering.
description: Deluta Black takes the conventions of blackletter type and injects a quirky, informal character suited to display and branding work. The single Black weight keeps it squarely in display territory, where the charm of the letterforms can be given room to breathe. A niche choice for projects that want gothic flavour without the usual severity.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Deluta Black as a fresh twist on tradition — fun, quirky and full of charm — positioning it as a playful and unique take on blackletter that invites experimentation in design.

name: Ekistra
classification_notes: Described as quirky and full of personality, Ekistra is a display-oriented sans aimed at playful, expressive contexts rather than text use, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A quirky display sans from Dharma Type aimed at playful, celebratory design work.
description: Lively and bold in character, Ekistra is built for contexts where personality takes precedence over neutrality. The foundry positions it toward party themes, joyful branding and upbeat graphic work. Details remain sparse, but the tone is squarely decorative rather than functional.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Ekistra as a quirky sans-serif made for happy, fun moments — lively, bold and full of personality, suited to playful designs, party themes or anything requiring a burst of joy.

name: Cigarette
classification_notes: The typeface is described as blending Art Deco elegance with unusual contrast, placing it firmly in the display category with an art deco subcategory. Its decorative and experimental nature makes it unsuitable for text use.
summary: A display typeface from Dharma Type drawing on Art Deco proportions with unusual stroke contrast and ligature support.
description: Built around an unusual contrast that the foundry describes as curious and intriguing, it takes Art Deco letter forms and pushes them toward something more contemporary. Ligatures are included for typographic refinement, making it suited to headlines, packaging and branding where a period-inflected decorative quality is needed.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Cigarette as an experimental typeface blending Art Deco elegance with a modern style. They note that its unusual contrast creates a curious and intriguing visual effect, adding character to any design, and that included ligatures offer enhanced typographic refinement and versatility.

name: Bistro Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface with decorative, display-oriented character, designed for signage and branding rather than extended text setting.
summary: A monospaced display face with a decorative personality, originally created for an Italian pub and restaurant.
description: Designed with signage, menus and branded materials in mind, Bistro Mono carries a distinctive character that sets it apart from utilitarian coding typefaces. Its origins in a single hospitality commission give it a specific visual register suited to personality-led branding. The monospacing adds a structured, even rhythm that works well at display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Bistro Mono as a monospaced font originally designed for a small Italian pub and restaurant, with a distinctive and eye-catching design suited to signage, menus and branding with personality.

name: Thousands
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a slab serif typeface; the extensive OpenType alternate system, including small caps and ligatures, places it firmly in the slab category with a versatile, system-oriented approach.
summary: A slab serif built around an extensive OpenType alternate system, allowing serifs, small caps and ligatures to shift the texture of a setting considerably.
description: The family draws its typographic range not from a large weight stack but from OpenType features: alternates reshape individual serifs, characters convert to small caps, and ligatures adjust the rhythm of running text. This makes Thousands unusual among slab serifs in that much of its expressive range is encoded within a compact set of fonts. Suited to editorial and branding work where typographic detail matters more than sheer weight variety.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Thousands as an incredibly versatile type system whose power grows through extensive OpenType alternates. Serifs adapt, characters transform into small caps, and ligatures enhance text flow, offering a rich and flexible typographic experience.

name: Camera
classification_notes: A sans-serif with Art Deco proportions and decorative qualities drawn from late 19th and early 20th century advertising typography, placing it firmly in the decorative/display end of the sans-serif spectrum.
summary: A vintage-flavoured sans-serif with Art Deco proportions, ligatures and small capitals suited to retro-inspired design work.
description: Drawn from the advertising typography of the late 1800s to early 20th century, it brings the decorative warmth of Art Deco into a legible, relatively simple sans. Ligatures and small capitals extend its usefulness into more considered layouts. A practical choice for packaging, branding or editorial work that calls for period character without heavy ornamentation.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Camera as a legible, simple and charming sans-serif inspired by Art Deco advertisements from the late 1800s to the early 20th century. They position it as a sweet and stylish typeface for retro-inspired projects, noting that its ligatures and small capitals make it suitable for advanced typography and elegant layouts.

name: Cotoris
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a glyphic sans serif, combining the structure of a sans with subtle incised or flared stroke endings rather than full serifs, placing it in the glyphic subcategory.
summary: A glyphic sans serif from Dharma Type with ligatures and small caps, built for refined, feminine design contexts.
description: A glyphic sans with incised detailing that lends it a more calligraphic warmth than a clean geometric or grotesque. Ligatures and small caps are included for richer typographic control. Recognised as a Rising Star on MyFonts in June 2007 shortly after release.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Cotoris as a beautiful glyphic sans serif designed with elegance and sophistication in mind, featuring ligatures and small caps for advanced typographic work, and suited to projects calling for a graceful, feminine touch.

name: Pansy Bo
classification_notes: Pansy Bo is a script typeface modelled on 19th-century handwriting styles, with an inky texture that simulates authentic pen-written letterforms, placing it firmly in the handwritten script subcategory.
summary: A 19th-century-inspired handwritten script with an inky texture that mimics the look of authentic pen calligraphy.
description: Modelled on 19th-century script styles, Pansy Bo replicates the uneven ink deposit and flowing strokes of period handwriting. The result carries an antique character well-suited to vintage branding, packaging and decorative design work. Its nostalgic quality sits naturally alongside historical imagery and heritage-oriented aesthetics.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Pansy Bo as based on 19th-century script styles, with an inky texture that gives it the appearance of authentic handwriting. They note its smooth, flowing strokes evoke nostalgia and antique charm, making it well suited to designs with a vintage or historical feel.

name: Daisy Lau
classification_notes: Daisy Lau is a script typeface directly modelled on 19th-century handwriting styles, featuring inky texture and flowing strokes characteristic of period penmanship.
summary: A 19th-century-inspired script with inky, handwritten texture suited to vintage and historical design work.
description: Modelled on 19th-century script styles, this typeface carries an inky, handwritten quality that suggests pen on paper rather than digital construction. Flowing strokes and organic variation in stroke weight give it an antique character well-suited to packaging, branding and editorial work that calls for period authenticity. A single-weight offering from Dharma Type, pitched squarely at vintage and nostalgia-driven projects.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Daisy Lau as based on 19th-century script styles, with an inky texture that gives it the appearance of authentic handwriting. They note its smooth, flowing strokes evoke nostalgia and antique charm, making it suited to vintage or historically themed designs.

name: Lily Wang
classification_notes: A 19th-century-inspired script with inky, flowing strokes characteristic of authentic period handwriting, placing it firmly in the handwritten script subcategory.
summary: A 19th-century-inspired handwritten script with an inky texture evoking period penmanship and antique charm.
description: Modelled on 19th-century script styles, Lily Wang carries an inky, textured quality that reads as authentic hand-lettering rather than a mechanical facsimile. Smooth, flowing strokes lend it a nostalgic warmth suited to vintage branding, packaging and historical editorial contexts. The single weight keeps the offering focused, relying on the authenticity of its letterforms rather than stylistic breadth.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Lily Wang as a font rooted in 19th-century script styles, with an inky texture that mimics authentic handwriting. They highlight its smooth, flowing strokes as conveying nostalgia and antique charm, positioning it for designs with a vintage or historical feel.

name: Machia
classification_notes: Machia is a handwritten script with inky, expressive strokes, placing it firmly in the script category as a casual or brush-influenced handwritten style.
summary: A handwritten script with full, inky strokes, recognised by MyFonts as a Rising Star in September 2006.
description: Machia draws on the qualities of pen-and-ink handwriting, with strokes that carry visible weight and fluid movement across letterforms. It was noted for its expressive, natural character when it received MyFonts' Rising Stars recognition. Well suited to packaging, editorial headers and any application where a personal, hand-drawn quality is required.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Machia as a handwritten script font overflowing with rich, inky strokes, and note that it was recognised as one of MyFonts' Rising Stars in September 2006, celebrated for its expressive and fluid character.

name: Machiarge
classification_notes: A brush script with baseball lettering characteristics, drawing on mid-20th-century American vernacular sign-painting and sports typography traditions.
summary: A baseball-style brush script drawn from mid-20th-century American sports and cinema lettering traditions.
description: Loose, energetic brush strokes recall the hand-lettered signage of American baseball culture and retro cinema title cards. The single style balances informal energy with period charm, making it well-suited to sports branding, poster work and packaging that calls for nostalgic American flair. Dharma Type list it among their best-sellers.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Machiarge as a best-selling baseball script featuring distinctive brush strokes inspired by the charm of 20th-century retro cinema, designed to evoke a nostalgic, energetic feel with a classic American flair.

name: Killernuts
classification_notes: Classified as display due to its wood type origins and highly expressive serifs rendered to resemble brush strokes; the serif treatment draws from Japanese Shodo calligraphy, placing it in decorative rather than conventional serif territory.
summary: A wood type display face whose serifs are drawn to resemble brush strokes, blending Western letterform structure with the gestural energy of Japanese Shodo calligraphy.
description: Built on wood type proportions, Killernuts replaces conventional serifs with forms that echo the tapered, expressive marks of Japanese brush calligraphy. The result is a Latin display face with an unusual cultural charge, suited to posters, logotypes and headline work where strong personality is required. The fusion of Eastern brushwork and Western wood type gives it a distinct visual tension that sets it apart from standard decorative serifs.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Killernuts as a wood type display font whose serifs resemble brush strokes, evoking the spirit of Japanese calligraphy (Shodo). They frame it as a striking fusion of Eastern tradition and Western typography, where East meets West in bold, expressive form.

name: Amsterdam Modern
classification_notes: The typeface draws on architectural forms and blends industrial precision with handcrafted warmth, placing it firmly in the display category with a geometric character suited to branding and packaging rather than extended text.
summary: A three-weight display face drawn from the stepped gable rooftops of Amsterdam's canal buildings, mixing industrial geometry with handcrafted warmth.
description: The letterforms take their cue from the distinctive silhouettes of Dutch canal-house rooftops, translating architectural rhythm into type. Three weights make it practical for logos, product packaging and short headline copy where character matters more than text economy. The blend of geometric structure and organic warmth gives it a point of difference from purely industrial display faces.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Amsterdam Modern as inspired by the distinctive rooftops of buildings lining the canals of Holland, blending an industrial aesthetic with handcrafted charm to create a balance of precision and warmth. Available in three weights, it is positioned for product packaging, logos and short text.

name: Celtics Modern
classification_notes: Rooted in insular half-uncial, the early medieval script tradition of Celtic manuscripts, this is best classified as blackletter given its historical script origins, though its handwritten character edges it toward display calligraphic styles.
summary: A modern reinterpretation of insular half-uncial script, drawing on early medieval Celtic manuscript lettering with a handwritten quality.
description: Taking insular half-uncial as its historical reference point, this face reproduces the rhythm and form of early Celtic manuscript lettering with a contemporary hand. The glyphs retain the spirit of ancient scribal work while avoiding strict historical reproduction, giving it flexibility for decorative and cultural-themed display use. Best suited to titles, branding or editorial contexts where a Celtic or medieval atmosphere is wanted without resorting to heavier blackletter traditions.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Celtics Modern as inspired by ancient Celtic lettering, particularly insular half-uncial, and position it as a modern reinterpretation of traditional forms. They note that its handwritten-style glyphs evoke the feeling of ancient script penned by hand, offering a fresh, contemporary touch to age-old letterforms.

name: Elpiedra
classification_notes: A rough-textured display face with deliberate distressing and worn aesthetics, placing it firmly in the grunge display subcategory.
summary: A rough, distressed display face with a deliberately worn texture suited to bold, confrontational design work.
description: Smokey in character and irregular in texture, it brings a raw, hand-worn quality to headlines, posters and packaging where a polished sans would feel out of place. The distressing is built into the letterforms rather than applied as an effect, giving it a consistent grit across settings. Works well in music, streetwear and subcultural contexts where authenticity of form matters.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Elpiedra as a smokey, rough-textured grunge font carrying a raw, rebellious spirit, suited to designs that need a bold, worn and fearless aesthetic.

name: Boycott
classification_notes: An expressive, distressed display typeface with rough, irregular letterforms designed for large-scale use such as posters and headlines. Fits squarely in the grunge display category due to its intentional roughness and raw character.
summary: A distressed display typeface with rough, irregular letterforms suited to posters and large headlines.
description: Awarded Second Prize at Best Font 2006 and named a New Rising Star on MyFonts, Boycott carries a raw, noisy character through subtle variations between its uppercase and lowercase letters. The deliberate roughness makes it well suited to poster work and editorial headlines where an aggressive, unpolished tone is required. A single weight keeps the offering focused on impact over versatility.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Boycott as a grungy display font that earned Second Prize at Best Font 2006 and was named a New Rising Star on MyFonts. The uppercase and lowercase letters feature subtle variations that add to its raw and expressive character, making it a fitting choice for posters and large headlines.

name: Talking Five
classification_notes: Classified as display due to its hand-drawn character and intended use in posters, packaging and children's materials rather than continuous text. The informal, rounded letterforms place it firmly in the hand-drawn sans subcategory.
summary: A hand-drawn sans serif aimed at children's publishing, toy packaging and school materials with a warm, informal character.
description: Soft, irregular letterforms give Talking Five an approachable quality suited to children's textbooks, activity materials and toy packaging. The hand-drawn construction avoids the rigidity of geometric or grotesque styles, lending layouts a casual warmth. A straightforward single-weight offering from Dharma Type for projects where friendliness is the primary requirement.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Talking Five as a hand-drawn sans serif font family that conveys a friendly and soft impression, calling it perfect for school activities, children's textbooks, posters and toy packaging, and noting that it brings a warm and approachable feel to any design.

name: Tokyotrail
classification_notes: Square, geometric letterforms with multi-directional strokes give this a constructed, architectural quality typical of display-only typefaces. It does not fit comfortably into any text category.
summary: A geometric display face inspired by Tokyo's urban grid, built from square forms with vertical, horizontal and diagonal strokes.
description: Designed with Tokyo's city structure as its starting point, Tokyotrail draws on the sprawling metropolis's layered transport and street grids to inform its letterforms. Square, angular geometry gives it a strong graphic presence suited to posters, signage and branding where impact at large sizes matters. The multi-directional stroke construction sets it apart from conventional geometric display types.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Tokyotrail as inspired by Japan's capital city, a metropolis of over 2,000 square kilometres. The design reflects the city's dynamic structure through lines running vertically, horizontally and diagonally, with square geometric forms that create a striking presence across a variety of design contexts.

name: Fault
classification_notes: Fault is a display typeface with decorative striped elements, placing it firmly in the experimental display category where visual texture takes precedence over legibility at small sizes.
summary: A display typeface with striped patterning built into the letterforms, aimed at poster and title work.
description: Striped elements run through the letterforms, giving each character a bold, graphic texture suited to large-scale applications. The treatment works best at headline sizes where the patterning reads clearly rather than collapsing into noise. Positioned for contemporary design projects where visual impact outweighs extended reading.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Fault as an experimental typeface with striped elements that add a bold and graphic edge, recommending it for eye-catching titles, posters and contemporary design projects.

name: Chic Hand
classification_notes: Chic Hand is a casual handwritten script with a warm, informal character; the presence of ligatures and a friendly aesthetic place it firmly in the casual script subcategory.
summary: A casual handwritten script with ligatures aimed at feminine and friendly design contexts.
description: Chic Hand is a handwritten-style script with ligatures, designed for a warm and approachable feel. It suits packaging, greeting cards, social media graphics and branding where an informal, personal tone is needed. The style leans distinctly feminine and cute, narrowing its range but sharpening its appeal within that niche.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Chic Hand as a handwritten-style font with ligatures, characterised by a warm, girly and cute touch, and suited to designs that call for a friendly and personal feel.

name: Jaguarundi
classification_notes: A heavily textured, scratched display face with no text utility; its worn, decadent aesthetic places it firmly in the distressed display category.
summary: A scratched and worn display face named after a Central and South American wild cat, aimed squarely at rock, grunge and underground poster work.
description: The face takes its name and spirit from the jaguarundi, a small wild cat of Central and South America, translating that rough, feral quality into a heavily distressed letterform. Its scratched and decadent texture suits posters, album art and flyer work where a raw, unglamorous edge is the point. A single style with no weights or italics keeps the offering tight and purposeful.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Jaguarundi as inspired by its namesake wild cat native to Central and South America, with a scratched, worn and decadent aesthetic. They position it as a perfect choice for rock, grunge or underground-themed posters.

name: Underconstructionism
classification_notes: Heavy, rugged letterforms inspired by construction site stencil signage place this firmly in the display category with a stencil subcategory.
summary: A heavy stencil display face drawn from the utilitarian signage found on construction sites, built for headlines and titles.
description: Thick, rugged letterforms channel the no-nonsense stencil lettering of construction site hoarding. The weight and visual density make it well-suited to display-size setting where impact matters more than finesse. A narrow use case, but it does what it sets out to do.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Underconstructionism as inspired by stencil signage commonly found on construction sites, with heavy, rugged letterforms that are bold and highly eye-catching, making it well suited to headlines and titles.

name: DoubleDagger
classification_notes: A geometric stencil typeface with clean, structured forms; the stencil cuts and geometric construction place it firmly in the display category rather than general-purpose sans-serif.
summary: A clean geometric stencil typeface spanning six weights with an accompanying dingbat set.
description: DoubleDagger is a geometric stencil family built around orthodox, regular forms that keep it legible across a wide range of applications. Six weights from Thin to Black give designers plenty of tonal range, while the Xtra subfamily adds a dingbat set for supplementary graphic elements. The clean stencil construction suits editorial, packaging and campaign work where a structured, industrial character is needed without excessive stylisation.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe DoubleDagger as a geometric stencil typeface available in a variety of weights, with a clean and orthodox design that makes it highly versatile and suitable for a wide range of creative projects. The family includes six weights plus an additional dingbat set under the Xtra subfamily.

name: Bebas
classification_notes: All-caps geometric sans designed primarily for headlines and titling, with a tall, condensed construction and clean monolinear strokes typical of display-oriented sans-serif lettering.
summary: A free all-caps display sans originally created in 2005 as a personal type design exercise, intended for headlines and titling.
description: Created by Ryoichi Tsunekawa of Dharma Type in 2005, Bebas is a tall, clean all-caps sans built for headlines and captions. It started as a practice project shared with the design community for feedback, and the original version has since been discontinued. Its geometric monolinear construction has made it one of the more widely recognised free display faces in circulation.
foundry_blurb: Dharma Type describe Bebas as a free font designed for headlines, captions, and titling, originally created in 2005 as a personal type design exercise to practise and receive feedback from the global design community. Development of the original version has since been discontinued.
